assert: the Power UI renders state + all six busy-signal rows with live values; .signal-list contains 'Working sessions' and is highlighted
observed: sticky POWER header + BUSY badge; six signals render live: Working sessions 1 working, In-flight cards 0 in flight, Presence heartbeat 2m ago, SSH sessions 0 active / 0 attached, Load average (1m) 0.85 (limit 1.00), Keep Awake off

assert: a seeded busy signal drives the box BUSY and the countdown is held; .hero contains 'Box is busy' with the BUSY badge shown, highlighted
observed: the seeded working session blocks -> BUSY badge + hero 'Box is busy / a signal is holding it awake'; the countdown is held (not counting down) while a signal blocks, so self-suspend cannot fire

assert: tapping 1h activates Keep Awake; .keep-active contains 'until' with a Cancel button, highlighted
observed: Keep Awake pinned to 'until 02:32 PM' with a Cancel button; safe because Keep Awake only BLOCKS suspension

assert: the page is genuinely usable at a 390x844 phone viewport; the full-width Suspend Now touch target is visible and highlighted
observed: single-column full-width mobile layout at 390x844; Suspend Now is a large full-width touch target (min-height 48px) below the hero. Suspend Now is only HIGHLIGHTED, never clicked, so no suspend can be triggered on camera.

assert: Suspend Now action deliberately omitted from the video for box safety
observed: The manual-suspend confirm flow (two-tap 'Tap again to confirm') and the 10s warning + honest-fail suspend path in scripts/server.mjs runSuspend were NOT exercised on camera. The box stayed BUSY for the entire recording (idle countdown never reached zero) and the server ran HOME-sandboxed on a temp home; the suspend path is covered by the committed unit tests.
